pattern: Collect each user's set of distinct action minutes, then tally how many users fall into each unique-minute-count bucket from 1 to k.
hints: For each user you only care about the number of distinct minutes they were active.
A set per user removes duplicate minutes automatically.
After computing every user's distinct-minute count, build a length-k array where index j-1 counts users with exactly j active minutes.

edge_inputs.description: same user all same minute gives UAM one
many distinct minutes for one user
k larger than any UAM leaves trailing zeros

stress.description: random logs over a bounded user and minute space with k covering max UAM
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    logs = [[rng.randrange(0, 50), rng.randrange(1, 40)] for _ in range(n)]
    from collections import defaultdict
    seen = defaultdict(set)
    for uid, t in logs:
        seen[uid].add(t)
    maxu = max(len(v) for v in seen.values())
    k = rng.randrange(maxu, 41)
    return [logs, k]
